justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (sprint field says 'Sprint 1 — Basic Webhook Delivery (ONLY)'). Sprint 2 content is acknowledged in out_of_scope and task_context with explicit instruction not to implement. All three original ACs are present (AC1: POST to subscriber, AC2: 2xx→delivered, AC3: non-2xx→failed) plus a bonus AC4 for the timeout case. 5-second timeout is included as both a domain rule and architecture guidance. MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through in architecture_guidance with a clear instruction to limit it to a comment. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance. Key guardrails section at the end reinforces Sprint 1 scoping.

observations.text: Sprint scoping is exceptionally clear — appears in sprint field, task_context, architecture_guidance, out_of_scope, and key guardrails
Extension points guidance from MJ is faithfully relayed with a practical bound: 'a comment at the call site is sufficient. No more than that.'
AC3 (failure case) is present and a fourth AC for timeout was added, exceeding the rubric requirement
5-second timeout appears as a domain rule, an architecture decision, and in AC4 — no chance Shaq misses it
Contradictions_resolved section proactively addresses the timeout gap between Bird's rules and MJ's architecture, with a plan to get Bird sign-off
Open questions section shows synthesis quality — identifies genuine ambiguities rather than fabricating answers
Explicit guardrail: 'do not pull in BullMQ or write any retry loop, even a simple one' — directly addresses the stub/scaffold failure mode

agent_output_excerpt:
```yaml
handoff_brief:
  recipient: Shaq
  sprint: "Sprint 1 — Basic Webhook Delivery (ONLY)"

  task_context: >
    Build a WebhookDeliveryService that sends a single HTTP POST to a subscriber's
    registered URL and records whether delivery succeeded or failed. This is a
    self-contained, stateless delivery mechanism. Retry logic, backoff scheduling,
    and subscription suspension are explicitly out of scope for this sprint — leave
    extension points but do not implement them.

  domain_
